evals.prompt: Je reprends ma review sur les inhibiteurs SGLT2 dans l'insuffisance cardiaque. Le dossier review/ contient déjà les résultats de recherche et le screening.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator reconstructs the execution tracker from existing review/ files, identifies that Gates 1-3a are passed, and resumes at the correct next phase (snowballing or extraction). It reads protocol.md to restore the research context.
expectations: The orchestrator prints an execution tracker with Gates 1, 2, and 3a marked as passed/completed
The orchestrator reads review/protocol.md and restores the research question about SGLT2 inhibitors in heart failure
The orchestrator identifies that the next phase is snowballing (Phase 3b) or extraction (Phase 4)
The orchestrator tells the user which phases are already done before proceeding
The orchestrator uses the PICO framework components from the protocol (Population: adults with HF, Intervention: SGLT2 inhibitors, etc.)
The orchestrator does NOT re-ask the user for the research question or inclusion criteria
The thin evidence warning is triggered (8 articles included, which is >= 5 so no warning expected) OR correctly not triggered

evals.prompt: Revue systématique sur l'efficacité des probiotiques dans la prévention des infections récurrentes à Clostridioides difficile.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator runs Phase 1 planning: defines a PICO framework, selects systematic review type, develops search strategy with >= 3 databases, sets inclusion/exclusion criteria, prints a complete protocol summary, and persists it to review/protocol.md.
expectations: The orchestrator uses PICO/PICOS framework (this is an intervention question)
The protocol includes Population (patients with recurrent C. difficile infection), Intervention (probiotics), Comparison (placebo/standard care), and Outcome
The review type is correctly identified as systematic review
At least 3 databases are listed in the search strategy
Search concepts include synonyms/MeSH terms for probiotics AND C. difficile
Inclusion and exclusion criteria are explicitly stated
The protocol is persisted to review/protocol.md via the Write tool

evals.prompt: Je voudrais faire une revue de littérature sur l'impact du microbiome intestinal sur la réponse aux immunothérapies dans le cancer du poumon.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator detects that review/ contains a protocol.md about rotator cuff comorbidities (different topic), flags the mismatch, and asks the user whether to start fresh or use a different directory.
expectations: The orchestrator detects existing review/ files (specifically protocol.md)
The orchestrator identifies that the existing protocol is about rotator cuff/comorbidities, not microbiome/immunotherapy/lung cancer
The orchestrator explicitly flags the topic mismatch to the user
The orchestrator asks the user whether to start fresh or use a different directory
The orchestrator does NOT silently overwrite or ignore the existing protocol

evals.prompt: On reprend la review scapulalgie. Le screening est terminé, il y a 12 articles inclus dans review/included_indices.json. Lance l'extraction.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator invokes litrev-extract on the 12 included articles, producing extracted_claims.json with quantitative and semantic claims, quality ratings, study designs, and theme assignments.
expectations: The orchestrator delegates to litrev-extract (not inline extraction)
extracted_claims.json is produced with entries for at least 8 of the 12 articles
Each article entry has claims (quantitative) and semantic_claims arrays
At least 15 total quantitative claims across all articles
At least 10 total semantic claims across all articles
All quantitative claims have a verbatim field with source text
Each article has a themes array with at least one theme assigned
The 2 degraded articles are handled: Beales_2023 (no abstract) gets NO_ABSTRACT or minimal claims, Rhon_2017 (no DOI) is still processed
Study design is assigned to each article (cohort, cross-sectional, SR/MA, RCT, etc.)
French-data articles (Bodin_2017, Bodin_2018) are present in the output

evals.prompt: L'extraction est terminée. extracted_claims.json contient 8 articles avec 22 claims quantitatives et 26 semantic claims sur 7 thèmes. Lance la synthèse.
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator invokes litrev-synthesize on the extracted claims, producing a thematic synthesis document with Pandoc citations, structured by themes, in French.
expectations: The orchestrator delegates to litrev-synthesize (not inline synthesis)
A review markdown file (*_review.md) is produced
The synthesis is organized by themes (at least 3 of the 7 available themes)
The synthesis uses Pandoc citation syntax [@key] referencing article keys
At least 1 citation per theme section (min_citation_density_per_theme)
The synthesis is written in French (synthesis_language: fr, matching protocol)
French-data articles (Bodin_2018, Bodin_2020) are cited in the synthesis
The synthesis includes a YAML header with title and metadata
Quantitative claims from extracted_claims.json are incorporated with numbers preserved
The synthesis does NOT fabricate statistics not present in extracted_claims.json

evals.prompt: La synthèse est terminée, le fichier review.md est prêt. Lance la vérification (Phase 6).
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator runs Phase 6 verification: verify_dois on the review, generate_bibliography to produce references.bib, audit_claims to cross-check claims against extracted_claims.json. Prints gate checkpoints for each step.
expectations: The orchestrator calls MCP tool verify_dois with review_path pointing to the review file
The orchestrator calls MCP tool generate_bibliography with review_path and output_path for references.bib
The orchestrator calls MCP tool audit_claims with review_path, claims_path (extracted_claims.json), and output_path (claims_audit.json)
Gate 6a checkpoint is printed: DOIs verified count, failed count, retracted count
Gate 6b checkpoint is printed: entries generated, mismatches, missing, extras
Gate 6c checkpoint is printed: total claims, verified, unverified, no-abstract, no-extraction
The orchestrator runs validate_gate(gate='6', review_dir='review/')
UNVERIFIED claims from audit_claims are investigated (not silently ignored)
If verify_dois reports failed DOIs, the orchestrator attempts correction (search CrossRef by title)

evals.prompt: La vérification est terminée (Phase 6 passée). Lance les audits de qualité (Phase 8).
evals.expected_output: The orchestrator launches two parallel agents: audit_fidelity and audit_methodology. Each reads its agent instructions from agents/ and produces a report. After both complete, the orchestrator deduplicates findings and runs interactive walkthroughs.
expectations: The orchestrator launches TWO agents in parallel (not sequential) using the Agent tool
Agent 1 reads agents/audit_fidelity.md instructions
Agent 2 reads agents/audit_methodology.md instructions
Both agents read review files from the review/ directory
Agent 1 produces review/audit_fidelity.md
Agent 2 produces review/audit_methodology.md
After both agents complete, the orchestrator checks for overlapping findings between the two reports
The orchestrator runs interactive walkthroughs sequentially (not in parallel)
Each finding is presented with options: ACCEPTED, NOTED, DEFERRED, REJECTED
The orchestrator runs validate_gate(gate='8', review_dir='review/')
